description: Zero-dependency asyncio NATS client for modern Python — JetStream, Key-Value, Object Store
rules: nc.subscribe() is SYNCHRONOUS and returns a Subscription — never write 'await nc.subscribe(...)' (that is nats-py's idiom, shown in the migration guide only for contrast)
Iterate a Subscription directly ('async for msg in sub:'); there is no '.messages' property (that is nats-py)
Use 'async with await natsio.connect(...)' for connection lifecycle; subscriptions are async context managers and async iterators
JetStream uses only the ADR-37 simplified API: consumer.fetch()/next()/consume() and stream.ordered_consumer(); there are no push consumers
In migration-guide code blocks, lines under a '# nats-py' comment are the OLD client's API for comparison — only lines under '# natsio' apply to this library
Install natsio[nkeys] for NKey or .creds authentication; the core package has zero runtime dependencies
Per-message TTLs and durations accept datetime.timedelta; whole-second ints and the string 'never' also work for TTLs
